CONTENT.Honours: SepahanIran Pro League: 2009–10Naft TehranHazfi Cup: 2016–17IranWAFF Championship: 2008
CONTENT.Club career: Alenemeh moved to Sepahan for the 2009–10 season, where he played as the club's main left-back. He moved to Shahin Bushehr the season after, and stayed there for a season before joining Tractor, where he help the club finish in second place. In the summer of 2012, Alenemeh joined Naft Tehran. In 2014 Alenemeh led Naft to a third place league finish and an Asian Champions League qualification.On 14 July 2014, Alenemeh returned to Tractor, signing a contract until 2019.
CONTENT.Career statistics: Last Update: 22 October 2014Scores and results list Iran's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Alenemeh goal.
CONTENT.International career: Alenemeh started playing for Iran in the 2008 WAFF Championship. On 1 June 2014, he was called into Iran's 2014 FIFA World Cup squad by Carlos Queiroz.

SUMMARY: Seyed Ahmad Alenemeh (Persian: احمد آل نعمه; born 20 October 1982) is an Iranian former professional footballer who played as a defender.
